_comment: Golden answers for the 8 C4 (Arundel Holdings / Calder Tower facilities coordination) compound questions. The engine keyword-scores the incoming question against 'keywords', then returns 'answer' with citations resolved+persona-trimmed. 'restricted_citations' are the subset trimmed for personas lacking access. 'tool' (optional) signals a Tools/write action. Hero action: update_entity on the work-order tracker.

golden.question: What did the last facilities coordination call decide about the Calder Tower chiller failure, and what actions were assigned?
golden.answer: The most recent Calder Tower facilities coordination call (10-JUN-2026, chaired by Marco Reyes) decided to: accept the suspected low-refrigerant suction-line leak as the working cause; authorise Coolflow to pressure-test, braze the leak, recharge and soak-test Chiller-2; deploy interim spot-cooling to floors 18-24 within 24 hours; and target full cooling restored by 26-JUN, with the tenant-notification commitment to be confirmed by leadership. Actions assigned: AI-101 — chiller leak repair + soak test — owner Sam Whitford (Coolflow), due 26-JUN; AI-102 — deploy interim spot-cooling to floors 18-24 — owner Aisha Bello, due 11-JUN; AI-103 — confirm the tenant-notification commitment with leadership approval — owner Raj Anand, due 11-JUN.

golden.question: What tenant-notification commitment did we make for the Calder Tower outage, and who approved it?
golden.answer: In the tenant-confidential thread (09-10 JUN), Tenant Relations (Raj Anand) flagged that the anchor tenant on floors 18-24 has lease cooling-availability obligations and asked what restoration time to formally communicate and what service-credit exposure to accept. Marco Reyes replied with the commitment — approved by Director Helen Stamos — to the anchor tenant: cooling restored by 26-JUN, interim spot-cooling on floors 18-24 within 24 hours, and acceptance of the contractual service-credit terms in the vendor SLA register if missed. The credit exposure is leadership-only.
golden.trimmed_answer: The Calder Tower tenant-notification commitment thread is tenant-confidential and restricted to facilities leadership, so the specific commitment and service-credit exposure cannot be shown to the active persona. At a non-restricted level: a tenant notification is being issued and an internal restoration plan targets cooling back by 26-JUN.

golden.question: Which work orders are open at Calder Tower and what do the building-ops channel posts say about progress on each?
golden.answer: Two work orders are open at Calder Tower per the tracker: WO-001 (Chiller-2 leak repair + soak test, owner Sam Whitford/Coolflow, due 26-JUN) and WO-002 (interim spot-cooling floors 18-24, owner Aisha Bello, due 11-JUN). The building-ops channel says: on WO-001, Coolflow is on site, the suction-line leak is located and being brazed, parts arrive tomorrow and it is tracking to 26-JUN restored; on WO-002, interim spot-cooling is deployed and running and the anchor tenant is comfortable for now. Both stay open until the chiller soak test is verified.

golden.question: What's the current status of the Calder Tower restoration across the last coordination call and the shared works log?
golden.answer: Current Calder Tower restoration status, consistent across two signals. From the last coordination call (10-JUN): working cause is the low-refrigerant suction-line leak; Coolflow authorised to repair and soak-test; interim spot-cooling deployed; target restored 26-JUN. From the shared works log: Chiller-2 repair is in progress with Coolflow brazing the leak, interim spot-cooling is live on floors 18-24, and the restored ETA is 26-JUN pending the soak test. Net: not yet restored — full cooling depends on completing the leak repair and the 24-hour soak test, ETA 26-JUN; the building is held on interim spot-cooling until then.

golden.question: I'm taking coverage on the Calder Tower chiller event — give me the current picture: last coordination-call decisions, suspected cause, contractor dispatched and ETA, open work orders and owners, and any tenant-notification commitment.
golden.answer:
Calder Tower chiller event — current picture:

1. Last coordination-call decisions (10-JUN): accept the low-refrigerant leak as working cause; authorise Coolflow to repair and soak-test; deploy interim spot-cooling to floors 18-24; target restored 26-JUN.

2. Suspected cause: low-refrigerant lockout on Chiller-2's lead compressor from a slow suction-line leak (Derek Owens, engineering).

3. Contractor dispatched and ETA: Coolflow (Sam Whitford) on site, Victor Hale brazing the leak; parts 11-JUN; soak-verified ETA 25-26 JUN.

4. Open work orders and owners: WO-001 chiller leak repair (Sam Whitford/Coolflow, due 26-JUN); WO-002 interim spot-cooling floors 18-24 (Aisha Bello, due 11-JUN).

5. Tenant-notification commitment: to the anchor tenant on floors 18-24 — cooling restored by 26-JUN, interim spot-cooling within 24 hours, and acceptance of the SLA service-credit terms if missed (approved by Helen Stamos).
golden.trimmed_answer:
Calder Tower chiller event — current picture (redacted for the active persona — tenant-confidential and vendor-commercial items withheld):

1. Last coordination-call decisions (10-JUN): accept the low-refrigerant leak as working cause; authorise Coolflow to repair and soak-test; deploy interim spot-cooling to floors 18-24; target restored 26-JUN.

2. Suspected cause: low-refrigerant lockout on Chiller-2 from a slow suction-line leak.

3. Contractor dispatched and ETA: Coolflow on site, leak being brazed; soak-verified ETA 25-26 JUN.

4. Open work orders and owners: WO-001 chiller leak repair (Coolflow, due 26-JUN); WO-002 interim spot-cooling floors 18-24 (Aisha Bello, due 11-JUN).

5. Tenant-notification commitment: withheld — restricted to facilities leadership.

- Bishop House — Coolflow Mechanical: overdue work order WO-003 (monthly AHU planned-maintenance window missed, CMMS PM-10233 Overdue); owner Carlos Pena — needs rebooking with the vendor.
